assertion_results.text: The answer recommends `monkeypatch` for controlling environment variables
assertion_results.evidence: Includes: "monkeypatch.setenv("APP_MODE", "value") to control the env var" and "monkeypatch.delenv("APP_MODE", raising=False) to simulate it missing"

assertion_results.text: The answer recommends `tmp_path` or an equivalent pytest temporary path fixture for isolated filesystem writes
assertion_results.evidence: Includes a dedicated section: "tmp_path (or tmp_path_factory) - Provides an isolated, per-test temporary directory for JSON output" and uses tmp_path in example tests

assertion_results.text: The answer keeps the strategy narrow and explicitly avoids relying on real environment state or repository files
assertion_results.evidence: Proposes only built-in fixtures focused on isolation: uses monkeypatch to set/del env vars and "monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path) to force writes into a temp dir" plus tmp_path for isolated writes; no reliance on real environment or repo paths is suggested
